name: app.R
content:
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Shiny app: Polynomial Regression vs Natural Splines — Interactive Explorer
# Run with:  shiny::runApp("app.R")
# Packages:  shiny, ggplot2, splines, dplyr, tidyr, patchwork
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

library(shiny)
library(ggplot2)
library(splines)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)

# ── Colour palette ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
COL_POLY   <- "#E74C3C"   # red
COL_SPLINE <- "#2980B9"   # blue
COL_TRUE   <- "#27AE60"   # green
COL_DATA   <- "grey60"
COL_KNOT   <- "#E67E22"   # orange

# ── UI ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ui <- fluidPage(
  tags$head(
    tags$style(HTML("
      body           { font-family: 'Segoe UI', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; }
      .sidebar-title { font-weight: bold; color: #1A5276; margin-bottom: 4px; }
      .info-box      { background: #EBF5FB; border-left: 4px solid #2980B9;
                       padding: 10px 14px; margin: 8px 0; border-radius: 3px; }
      .warn-box      { background: #FEF9E7; border-left: 4px solid #F39C12;
                       padding: 10px 14px; margin: 8px 0; border-radius: 3px; }
      .key-box       { background: #EAFAF1; border-left: 4px solid #27AE60;
                       padding: 10px 14px; margin: 8px 0; border-radius: 3px; }
      h4             { color: #1A5276; }
      .nav-tabs > li.active > a { color: #1A5276; font-weight: bold; }
    "))
  ),

  titlePanel(
    div(
      h2("Polynomial Regression vs Natural Splines",
         style = "color:#1A5276; margin-bottom:2px;"),
      p("An interactive explorer for Statistics and Big Data Analysis — MBG",
        style = "color:grey; margin-top:0; font-size:13px;")
    )
  ),

  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      width = 3,

      # ── Data controls ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
      div(class = "sidebar-title", "Data"),
      selectInput("true_fn", "True relationship:",
        choices = c(
          "sin(x)"                  = "sin",
          "x² (quadratic)"          = "quad",
          "Step function"           = "step",
          "Smooth + local spike"    = "spike",
          "Nearly linear"           = "linear"
        ), selected = "sin"),
      sliderInput("n_obs", "Number of observations:",
                  min = 50, max = 400, value = 200, step = 50),
      sliderInput("noise_sd", "Noise (SD):",
                  min = 0.05, max = 1.0, value = 0.30, step = 0.05),
      actionButton("reseed", "New sample", class = "btn btn-sm btn-default",
                   icon = icon("refresh")),
      hr(),

      # ── Polynomial controls ────────────────────────────────────────────
      div(class = "sidebar-title", "Polynomial fit"),
      checkboxInput("show_poly", "Show polynomial fit", value = TRUE),
      conditionalPanel("input.show_poly",
        sliderInput("poly_degree", "Degree:",
                    min = 1, max = 12, value = 4, step = 1),
        checkboxInput("poly_raw", "Raw (not orthogonal) polynomials", value = FALSE)
      ),
      hr(),

      # ── Spline controls ────────────────────────────────────────────────
      div(class = "sidebar-title", "Natural spline fit"),
      checkboxInput("show_spline", "Show natural spline fit", value = TRUE),
      conditionalPanel("input.show_spline",
        sliderInput("spline_df", "Degrees of freedom (deg_free):",
                    min = 2, max = 14, value = 4, step = 1),
        checkboxInput("show_knots", "Show knot positions", value = TRUE)
      ),
      hr(),

      # ── Display controls ───────────────────────────────────────────────
      div(class = "sidebar-title", "Display"),
      checkboxInput("show_true", "Show true function", value = TRUE),
      checkboxInput("show_ci",   "Show 95% confidence bands", value = FALSE)
    ),

    mainPanel(
      width = 9,
      tabsetPanel(id = "tabs",

        # ── Tab 1: Fit comparison ────────────────────────────────────────
        tabPanel("Fit Comparison",
          br(),
          plotOutput("fitPlot", height = "420px"),
          br(),
          uiOutput("fitMetrics")
        ),

        # ── Tab 2: Basis functions ───────────────────────────────────────
        tabPanel("Basis Functions",
          br(),
          div(class = "info-box",
            strong("What are basis functions?"),
            " The model replaces a single predictor x with multiple new columns —
            one per basis function. The linear model then finds the best weighted
            combination of these curves. Each column in the plot below is one
            such basis function."
          ),
          fluidRow(
            column(6,
              h4("Natural spline basis (step_ns)"),
              plotOutput("splineBasisPlot", height = "320px")
            ),
            column(6,
              h4("Polynomial basis (step_poly)"),
              plotOutput("polyBasisPlot", height = "320px")
            )
          ),
          br(),
          div(class = "key-box",
            strong("Key insight:"),
            " Natural spline basis functions are local — each one is large in
            a restricted region of x and near-zero elsewhere. Polynomial basis
            functions (even orthogonal ones) extend across the entire range,
            which is why changing the fit in one region affects the whole curve."
          )
        ),

        # ── Tab 3: Bias-variance explorer ───────────────────────────────
        tabPanel("Bias–Variance Explorer",
          br(),
          div(class = "info-box",
            strong("What does this tab show?"),
            " Draws multiple independent samples from the same data-generating
            process and fits the model to each. Narrow spread = low variance.
            Deviation from the true function = bias."
          ),
          sliderInput("n_sim", "Number of simulated datasets:",
                      min = 5, max = 30, value = 15, step = 5),
          plotOutput("bvPlot", height = "440px"),
          br(),
          div(class = "warn-box",
            strong("Observation:"),
            " Increase the polynomial degree to 10 or 12 and watch what
            happens to the spread of fits near the boundaries — this is
            high variance. The natural spline with the same number of df
            stays much more stable."
          )
        ),

        # ── Tab 4: Knot placement ────────────────────────────────────────
        tabPanel("Knot Placement",
          br(),
          div(class = "info-box",
            strong("How do knots control flexibility?"),
            " Each interior knot adds one extra basis function (one extra degree
            of freedom). Knots are placed at evenly-spaced quantiles of x by
            default in step_ns(). The plots below show what happens as you
            add more knots."
          ),
          plotOutput("knotPlot", height = "500px")
        ),

        # ── Tab 5: RMSE comparison ───────────────────────────────────────
        tabPanel("Model Comparison",
          br(),
          div(class = "info-box",
            "In-sample R² and RMSE for the current data and settings. Use
            the sliders to explore how these metrics change with model
            complexity. Remember: in-sample metrics always improve with
            more df — use cross-validation (see the Quarto notebook) to
            estimate out-of-sample performance."
          ),
          plotOutput("rmseBarPlot", height = "360px"),
          br(),
          tableOutput("metricsTable")
        )
      )
    )
  )
)

# ── Server ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
server <- function(input, output, session) {

  # ── Reactive seed (for "New sample" button) ─────────────────────────────
  seed_val <- reactiveVal(123)
  observeEvent(input$reseed, { seed_val(sample.int(1e5, 1)) })

  # ── True function helper ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  true_fn <- function(x, fn) {
    switch(fn,
      sin    = sin(x),
      quad   = 0.04 * (x - 5)^2 - 1,
      step   = ifelse(x < 5, -0.5, 0.5) + 0.1 * x,
      spike  = sin(x) + 1.5 * exp(-5 * (x - 7)^2),
      linear = 0.3 * x - 1.2
    )
  }

  # ── Reactive data ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  dat <- reactive({
    set.seed(seed_val())
    n  <- input$n_obs
    x  <- seq(0, 10, length.out = n)
    y  <- true_fn(x, input$true_fn) + rnorm(n, sd = input$noise_sd)
    data.frame(x = x, y = y)
  })

  # ── Fit models & extract predictions ────────────────────────────────────
  model_fits <- reactive({
    df <- dat()
    x_grid <- seq(0, 10, length.out = 400)

    # Polynomial
    poly_fit <- tryCatch({
      poly_type <- if (input$poly_raw) "raw = TRUE" else "raw = FALSE"
      lm(y ~ poly(x, input$poly_degree, raw = input$poly_raw), data = df)
    }, error = function(e) NULL)

    # Natural spline
    ns_fit <- tryCatch({
      lm(y ~ ns(x, df = input$spline_df), data = df)
    }, error = function(e) NULL)

    # Prediction grid with SE
    pred_df <- data.frame(x = x_grid)
    pred_df$true_y <- true_fn(x_grid, input$true_fn)

    if (!is.null(poly_fit)) {
      p <- predict(poly_fit, newdata = pred_df, interval = "confidence", level = 0.95)
      pred_df$poly_fit  <- p[, "fit"]
      pred_df$poly_lo   <- p[, "lwr"]
      pred_df$poly_hi   <- p[, "upr"]
    }

    if (!is.null(ns_fit)) {
      p <- predict(ns_fit, newdata = pred_df, interval = "confidence", level = 0.95)
      pred_df$ns_fit    <- p[, "fit"]
      pred_df$ns_lo     <- p[, "lwr"]
      pred_df$ns_hi     <- p[, "upr"]
    }

    list(
      data       = df,
      pred       = pred_df,
      poly_fit   = poly_fit,
      ns_fit     = ns_fit,
      knot_pos   = if (!is.null(ns_fit)) attr(ns(df$x, df = input$spline_df), "knots") else NULL
    )
  })

  # ── Tab 1: Fit comparison plot ───────────────────────────────────────────
  output$fitPlot <- renderPlot({
    res  <- model_fits()
    df   <- res$data
    pred <- res$pred

    p <- ggplot(df, aes(x, y)) +
      geom_point(colour = COL_DATA, size = 1.2, alpha = 0.6) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
            legend.position = "bottom") +
      labs(title = "Polynomial regression vs natural spline fit",
           subtitle = paste0("True function: ", input$true_fn,
                             "    |    n = ", input$n_obs,
                             "    |    noise SD = ", input$noise_sd),
           x = "x", y = "y")

    if (input$show_true) {
      p <- p + geom_line(data = pred, aes(x, true_y, colour = "True function"),
                         linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dashed")
    }

    if (input$show_poly && "poly_fit" %in% names(pred)) {
      if (input$show_ci) {
        p <- p + geom_ribbon(data = pred, aes(x, ymin = poly_lo, ymax = poly_hi),
                             fill = COL_POLY, alpha = 0.15, inherit.aes = FALSE)
      }
      p <- p + geom_line(data = pred, aes(x, poly_fit, colour = paste0("Polynomial (deg=", input$poly_degree, ")")),
                         linewidth = 1.1)
    }

    if (input$show_spline && "ns_fit" %in% names(pred)) {
      if (input$show_ci) {
        p <- p + geom_ribbon(data = pred, aes(x, ymin = ns_lo, ymax = ns_hi),
                             fill = COL_SPLINE, alpha = 0.15, inherit.aes = FALSE)
      }
      p <- p + geom_line(data = pred, aes(x, ns_fit, colour = paste0("Natural spline (df=", input$spline_df, ")")),
                         linewidth = 1.1)
    }

    # Knot positions
    if (input$show_spline && input$show_knots && !is.null(res$knot_pos)) {
      p <- p + geom_vline(xintercept = res$knot_pos,
                          linetype = "dotted", colour = COL_KNOT, linewidth = 0.7)
    }

    # Colour scale
    colour_vals <- c("True function" = COL_TRUE)
    if (input$show_poly)
      colour_vals[paste0("Polynomial (deg=", input$poly_degree, ")")] <- COL_POLY
    if (input$show_spline)
      colour_vals[paste0("Natural spline (df=", input$spline_df, ")")] <- COL_SPLINE

    p + scale_colour_manual(values = colour_vals, name = "")
  })

  # ── Tab 1: Metrics summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────
  output$fitMetrics <- renderUI({
    res <- model_fits()
    df  <- res$data

    metric_for <- function(fit, label) {
      if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
      yhat   <- fitted(fit)
      rss    <- sum((df$y - yhat)^2)
      tss    <- sum((df$y - mean(df$y))^2)
      r2     <- 1 - rss / tss
      rmse   <- sqrt(mean((df$y - yhat)^2))
      div(class = "info-box", style = "display:inline-block; min-width:220px; margin-right:12px;",
        strong(label), br(),
        "RMSE = ", strong(round(rmse, 4)), br(),
        "R²   = ", strong(round(r2, 4)),
        br(),
        em(style = "font-size:11px; color:grey;", "(training data only — use CV for unbiased estimates)")
      )
    }

    label_poly   <- paste0("Polynomial (degree = ", input$poly_degree, ")")
    label_spline <- paste0("Natural spline (df = ", input$spline_df, ")")

    tagList(
      if (input$show_poly)   metric_for(res$poly_fit, label_poly),
      if (input$show_spline) metric_for(res$ns_fit,   label_spline)
    )
  })

  # ── Tab 2: Basis function plots ──────────────────────────────────────────
  output$splineBasisPlot <- renderPlot({
    df_val <- input$spline_df
    x_seq  <- seq(0, 10, length.out = 300)
    basis  <- ns(x_seq, df = df_val)
    colnames(basis) <- paste0("B", seq_len(df_val))
    bdf <- as.data.frame(basis)
    bdf$x <- x_seq
    blong <- pivot_longer(bdf, -x, names_to = "basis_fn", values_to = "value")

    pal <- scales::hue_pal()(df_val)

    ggplot(blong, aes(x, value, colour = basis_fn)) +
      geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
      scale_colour_manual(values = pal, name = "Basis\nfunction") +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      labs(subtitle = paste0("deg_free = ", df_val),
           x = "x", y = "Basis value")
  })

  output$polyBasisPlot <- renderPlot({
    d     <- input$poly_degree
    x_seq <- seq(0, 10, length.out = 300)
    basis <- poly(x_seq, d, raw = input$poly_raw)
    colnames(basis) <- paste0("P", seq_len(d))
    bdf <- as.data.frame(basis)
    bdf$x <- x_seq
    blong <- pivot_longer(bdf, -x, names_to = "basis_fn", values_to = "value")

    pal <- scales::hue_pal()(d)

    ggplot(blong, aes(x, value, colour = basis_fn)) +
      geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
      scale_colour_manual(values = pal, name = "Basis\nfunction") +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      labs(subtitle = paste0("degree = ", d,
                             if (input$poly_raw) " (raw)" else " (orthogonal)"),
           x = "x", y = "Basis value")
  })

  # ── Tab 3: Bias-variance explorer ───────────────────────────────────────
  output$bvPlot <- renderPlot({
    n_sim <- input$n_sim
    n     <- input$n_obs
    x_seq <- seq(0, 10, length.out = 300)
    true_y <- true_fn(x_seq, input$true_fn)

    # Generate many datasets and fit both models
    sim_results <- lapply(seq_len(n_sim), function(i) {
      set.seed(seed_val() + i)
      x <- seq(0, 10, length.out = n)
      y <- true_fn(x, input$true_fn) + rnorm(n, sd = input$noise_sd)
      dfi <- data.frame(x = x, y = y)

      poly_p <- tryCatch(
        predict(lm(y ~ poly(x, input$poly_degree, raw = input$poly_raw), data = dfi),
                newdata = data.frame(x = x_seq)),
        error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, length(x_seq))
      )

      ns_p <- tryCatch(
        predict(lm(y ~ ns(x, df = input$spline_df), data = dfi),
                newdata = data.frame(x = x_seq)),
        error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, length(x_seq))
      )

      data.frame(x = x_seq, poly = poly_p, ns = ns_p, sim = i)
    })

    sim_df <- bind_rows(sim_results)

    p1 <- ggplot() +
      geom_line(data = sim_df, aes(x, poly, group = sim),
                colour = COL_POLY, alpha = 0.35, linewidth = 0.5) +
      geom_line(data = data.frame(x = x_seq, y = true_y),
                aes(x, y), colour = COL_TRUE, linewidth = 1.2, linetype = "dashed") +
      coord_cartesian(ylim = c(min(true_y) - 2.5, max(true_y) + 2.5)) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      labs(title = paste0("Polynomial (degree = ", input$poly_degree, ")"),
           subtitle = paste0(n_sim, " datasets; green dashed = true function"),
           x = "x", y = "y")

    p2 <- ggplot() +
      geom_line(data = sim_df, aes(x, ns, group = sim),
                colour = COL_SPLINE, alpha = 0.35, linewidth = 0.5) +
      geom_line(data = data.frame(x = x_seq, y = true_y),
                aes(x, y), colour = COL_TRUE, linewidth = 1.2, linetype = "dashed") +
      coord_cartesian(ylim = c(min(true_y) - 2.5, max(true_y) + 2.5)) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
      labs(title = paste0("Natural spline (df = ", input$spline_df, ")"),
           subtitle = paste0(n_sim, " datasets; green dashed = true function"),
           x = "x", y = "y")

    p1 + p2
  })

  # ── Tab 4: Knot placement ───────────────────────────────────────────────
  output$knotPlot <- renderPlot({
    df_vals <- c(2, 3, 5, 8)
    df_data <- dat()
    x_grid  <- seq(0, 10, length.out = 400)

    plots <- lapply(df_vals, function(dv) {
      fit  <- lm(y ~ ns(x, df = dv), data = df_data)
      knots <- attr(ns(df_data$x, df = dv), "knots")
      pred  <- predict(fit, newdata = data.frame(x = x_grid))
      true_y <- true_fn(x_grid, input$true_fn)

      base_df <- data.frame(x = x_grid, y_hat = pred, true_y = true_y)

      p <- ggplot(df_data, aes(x, y)) +
        geom_point(colour = COL_DATA, size = 0.7, alpha = 0.5) +
        geom_line(data = base_df, aes(x, true_y, colour = "True"), linewidth = 0.7, linetype = "dashed") +
        geom_line(data = base_df, aes(x, y_hat, colour = "Spline"), linewidth = 1) +
        geom_vline(xintercept = knots, linetype = "dotted", colour = COL_KNOT, linewidth = 0.6) +
        scale_colour_manual(values = c(True = COL_TRUE, Spline = COL_SPLINE), guide = "none") +
        coord_cartesian(ylim = range(df_data$y) * 1.1) +
        theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
        labs(title = paste0("deg_free = ", dv, "  (", max(0, dv - 2), " interior knots)"),
             x = "x", y = "y")
      p
    })

    (plots[[1]] + plots[[2]]) / (plots[[3]] + plots[[4]])
  })

  # ── Tab 5: RMSE bar chart ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  output$rmseBarPlot <- renderPlot({
    df     <- dat()
    df_seq <- 2:10

    rmse_ns   <- sapply(df_seq, function(dv) {
      fit  <- tryCatch(lm(y ~ ns(x, df = dv), data = df), error = function(e) NULL)
      if (is.null(fit)) return(NA)
      sqrt(mean(residuals(fit)^2))
    })

    rmse_poly <- sapply(df_seq, function(dv) {
      fit <- tryCatch(lm(y ~ poly(x, dv, raw = FALSE), data = df), error = function(e) NULL)
      if (is.null(fit)) return(NA)
      sqrt(mean(residuals(fit)^2))
    })

    cmp_df <- data.frame(
      df    = rep(df_seq, 2),
      rmse  = c(rmse_ns, rmse_poly),
      model = rep(c("Natural spline", "Polynomial"), each = length(df_seq))
    )

    ggplot(cmp_df, aes(x = factor(df), y = rmse, fill = model)) +
      geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7, alpha = 0.85) +
      geom_vline(xintercept = which(df_seq == input$spline_df) - 0.5,
                 linetype = "dashed", colour = COL_SPLINE, linewidth = 0.7) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = c("Natural spline" = COL_SPLINE, "Polynomial" = COL_POLY),
                        name = "Model") +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
      theme(legend.position = "bottom",
            plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")) +
      labs(title    = "Training RMSE across degrees of freedom / polynomial degree",
           subtitle = "Warning: training RMSE always decreases with complexity — use CV for fair comparison",
           x        = "Degrees of freedom / degree",
           y        = "Training RMSE")
  })

  output$metricsTable <- renderTable({
    df <- dat()

    calc_metrics <- function(fit, label) {
      if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
      yhat <- fitted(fit)
      rss  <- sum((df$y - yhat)^2)
      tss  <- sum((df$y - mean(df$y))^2)
      data.frame(
        Model     = label,
        RMSE      = round(sqrt(mean((df$y - yhat)^2)), 4),
        R_squared = round(1 - rss / tss, 4),
        Df        = fit$rank - 1
      )
    }

    res <- model_fits()
    bind_rows(
      calc_metrics(res$poly_fit, paste0("Polynomial (degree = ", input$poly_degree, ")")),
      calc_metrics(res$ns_fit,   paste0("Natural spline (df = ", input$spline_df, ")"))
    )
  }, striped = TRUE, hover = TRUE, bordered = TRUE)
}

# ── Run ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)
